name: hcportal_deleteEvent
description: Soft-deletes an event by setting Event.removed=1 and Event.updatedAt=GETDATE(), but only if no hashers have a checked-in attendance state (HasherEventMap.AttendenceState >= 20). Returns a plain Result string — callers must read the message text to distinguish success from blocked deletion. Does not throw an error rowset when deletion is blocked; only the ErrorEnvelope is returned on auth/validation failure.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Auth: device ID used to look up the authenticated user and device secret. Validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token, validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth with @publicEventId as the context identifier.

parameters.name: publicEventId
parameters.description: Required: identifies the event to soft-delete. Changed from NVARCHAR(250) in HC5 to UNIQUEIDENTIFIER. Validated for NULL after auth check.

rowsets.name: ErrorEnvelope
rowsets.description: Returned on auth failure, NULL publicEventId, event not found, or runtime error. Short-circuits on first error.

rowsets.name: DeletionResult
rowsets.description: Returned after validation passes (event found). Single row. Returned for both successful deletion and blocked deletion — callers must read the Result string to determine outcome.

columns.name: Result
columns.description: Outcome message. 'This run has been successfully removed' on success. 'Cannot delete this run because N Hasher(s) have been checked in to the run.' when AttendenceState >= 20 for one or more attendees. 'Event not found or already removed' if @@ROWCOUNT=0 after UPDATE (concurrent deletion edge case).

sideEffects: UPDATE HC.Event SET removed=1, updatedAt=GETDATE() WHERE id=@eventId — only when @countAtHash = 0 (no checked-in attendees)
breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change the AttendenceState >= 20 threshold without coordinating with the portal client
Never change the Result message strings without coordinating with callers that parse them
